title: Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Dhyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary in some sources) is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Buldhana district of the Indian state of Maharasht
overview.description: Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary (also spelled Dhyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary in some sources) is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Buldhana district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. The sanctuary is part of Melghat Tiger Reserve and is located near the Dnyanganga river, a tributary of the Tapti. The sanctuary was notified by the Government of Maharashtra in 1997 under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 and covers an area of approximately 205.20 km2. It lies within the Vidarbha region of Maharashtra and forms an important protected forest tract supporting regional biodiversity and wildlife mov

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jalgaon Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Aurangabad Railway Station (133 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dnyanganga Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dnyanganga Dam.
